neighborhoods.name: Greenbrier
neighborhoods.description: Major commercial and retail hub in central Chesapeake with shopping centers, restaurants, and a mix of single-family homes and apartment complexes along Greenbrier Parkway
neighborhoods.character: Suburban commercial, retail-oriented, convenient, moderate density

housing.predominantType: Single-family homes, townhomes, and apartment complexes near retail centers

neighborhoods.character_notes: Most convenient area for errands and shopping. Greenbrier Mall, Sam's Club, Target, multiple grocery stores all nearby. Good restaurant selection. Easy access to I-64 and Chesapeake Expressway. Medical facilities nearby.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living Chesapeake VA

neighborhoods.name: Great Bridge
neighborhoods.description: Historic area in southern Chesapeake centered around the Great Bridge Lock and the Intracoastal Waterway, with a charming small-town village center and surrounding suburban neighborhoods
neighborhoods.character: Historic small-town, waterfront, family-oriented, quieter suburban

housing.predominantType: Single-family homes on larger lots, some waterfront properties, newer subdivisions

neighborhoods.character_notes: Historic Battlefield Park commemorating the 1775 Battle of Great Bridge. Small village center with local shops and restaurants. Popular with boaters due to Intracoastal Waterway access. Good schools. Quieter and more rural-feeling than northern Chesapeake.

sources.title: City of Chesapeake - Great Bridge Battlefield

neighborhoods.name: Deep Creek
neighborhoods.description: Northwestern Chesapeake neighborhood bordering Norfolk and Portsmouth, with a more urban feel, mixed housing, and proximity to the Great Dismal Swamp
neighborhoods.character: Urban-suburban transition, working-class, diverse, close to Norfolk

housing.predominantType: Older single-family homes, duplexes, and modest apartment complexes

neighborhoods.character_notes: Most affordable area of Chesapeake. Close to Great Dismal Swamp NWR trailheads. Easy access to Portsmouth and Norfolk via I-664 and US-17. More diverse and working-class than southern Chesapeake. Deep Creek Lock Park along the Dismal Swamp Canal.

sources.title: Great Dismal Swamp NWR - US Fish & Wildlife

neighborhoods.name: Western Branch
neighborhoods.description: Established suburban neighborhood in northern Chesapeake known for good schools, parks, and quiet residential streets with convenient access to I-664 and Suffolk

housing.predominantType: Single-family homes in established subdivisions, some newer townhome developments

neighborhoods.character_notes: Quiet, well-established suburban community. Western Branch Park has trails, sports fields, and a community center. Good access to I-664 for commuting. Nearby Bennett's Creek commercial area has grocery stores and restaurants. Strong neighborhood associations.

neighborhoods.name: South Norfolk / Edinburgh
neighborhoods.description: Historic urban neighborhood in the northernmost part of Chesapeake, originally an independent city, with a walkable main street and proximity to Norfolk
neighborhoods.character: Historic urban, walkable core, revitalizing, diverse

housing.predominantType: Historic bungalows, row houses, older single-family homes, and some newer infill

neighborhoods.character_notes: Most walkable part of Chesapeake with a small-town urban feel. Historic district with early 1900s architecture. Revitalizing with new restaurants and small businesses. Elizabeth River access. Best HRT bus service in Chesapeake. Close to Norfolk and Portsmouth.
